description_long: This is a historic, wall-mounted stone drinking fountain set against a brick backdrop. The structure features a tall, rectangular stone slab with an arched top, inscribed with faded gold lettering. At the lower center, a shallow, semi-circular basin projects outward, designed to collect and channel water. The base is broad and solid, resting on a stone platform with visible weathering and moss growth, indicating outdoor placement. The sides are thick and robust, with a plaque or plate visible on one side, and the rear is completely flat and black, likely to be flush with a wall. The overall appearance is utilitarian yet decorative, typical of public drinking fountains found in parks or along city streets.
description: A historic, wall-mounted stone drinking fountain with an arched top, inscribed face, and a shallow semi-circular basin set against a brick wall.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched stone face, inscribed lettering, and the projecting semi-circular basin.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the stone structure and a visible plaque or plate on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a flat, featureless black surface, likely intended to be flush against a wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and the stone platform base.
